title: Corporate Internal Auditor, Specialist
description: Embrace the opportunity to become a Corporate Internal Auditor Specialist and play a key role in executing financial, operational, and IT audits. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, ensure SOX 404 compliance, and contribute to a strong internal control environment. Fluency in English and Japanese is essential. Grow your career with a global leader in audit services.

title: [Innovative Medicine] Director, Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific)
description:
At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function: 

Legal & Compliance

Job Sub Function: 

Enterprise Compliance

Job Category:

People Leader

All Job Posting Locations:

Shizuoka, Japan

Job Description:

Job DescriptionAbout Innovative Medicine 

Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow.

Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way.

Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative-medicine

Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific)

Johnson & Johnson is the world's most comprehensive and broadly-based healthcare Company, touching the lives of nearly a billion people every day.  Our Family of Companies throughout the world compete in pharmaceutical, and medical devices and diagnostics markets and have the skills and resources to tackle the world's most pressing health issues.  We are currently recruiting for Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific) to be located in Japan.

DESCRIPTION

The Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific), reports directly to the Senior Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific).

Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific) will be a member of the J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific) team and a member of the Innovative Medicines Regulatory Compliance (IMRC) team within the JJRC organization.  The J&J Regulatory Compliance Director (Asia Pacific) will be accountable for regulatory compliance of the Innovative Medicines Supply Chain sites in Asia Pacific.  He/she will be responsible for compliance oversight and execution of key compliance programs to drive proactive and sustained compliance to enable compliant delivery of business objectives.  The Director, J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific) will lead a team(s) of Compliance professionals to execute tactical compliance activities at the supply chain sites in the region.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Serve as an expert regulatory compliance advisor and Subject Matter Expert on key strategic and critical compliance issues or activities for the Asia Pacific region.
Understand site risk profiles and proactively identify and prioritize risks.  Partner with stakeholders to escalate critical risks to the appropriate levels of management.
Provide compliance expertise and regional compliance perspective in the development of risk mitigation strategies.  Make decisions regarding the applicability of relevant Health Authorities regulatory requirements.
Lead a team(s) of compliance professionals to provide compliance oversight and execute compliance programs to ensure compliance with all voluntary, regulatory, and statutory standards and requirements.
Support successful product realization and advice businesses and Supply Chain on compliance strategies. Understands compliance risk profile, proactively identify, communicate and prioritize compliance risks. Partner with stakeholders to escalate critical risks to the appropriate levels of management and support the mitigation of compliance risk.
Serve as a Subject Matter Expert to support proactive or reactive responses to issues including on-site support during regulatory agency inspections as well as review responses to regulatory agency inspections.
Understand and communicate emerging external regulatory compliance issues and trends. actions.
Partner with the stakeholders to identify, prioritize and ensure implementation of new and revised regulatory requirements and expectations. Support implementation plans for regulatory requirement changes including routinely monitoring and verifying of implementation activities.
Establish organizational talent strategy to include goals and objectives, performance and development, career and succession planning.
Accountable for performance management, personnel development and timely completion of 5 Conversations.
Establish a high performance and continuous improvement culture driven by key performance metrics, benchmarking and sharing of best practices.
As applicable, conduct and/or facilitate assessments e.g. pre-PAI audits, Due Diligence audits, Gap analysis, to support business needs and assess compliance risk for the organization.
Represents J&J Regulatory Compliance (Asia Pacific) in applicable Q&C and JJRC LT Forums and Councils.
Provide regulatory compliance support to ad hoc committees and projects as necessary.
 

REQUIREMENTS

Bachelor’s degree in science or related field is required.
Graduate degree/studies in science, engineering, business, organizational management, or equivalent experience preferred.
Minimum of 10 years of experience in Quality Assurance, Operations, and/or Regulatory Compliance is required.
Experience in managing various aspects of Quality and Regulatory Compliance in an international / global regulated healthcare environment is required.
Experience working in the Pharmaceutical, Medical Device, or Consumer regulated healthcare environment is required.
Detailed knowledge of Pharmaceutical regulatory requirements is required.  Knowledge of Asia Pacific regulatory requirements is an advantage.
Experience working with the US FDA, EMA and other foreign regulatory agencies is preferred.
Demonstrated track record of achieving successful compliance outcomes, remediating compliance issues and supporting regulatory agency inspections is required. 
Ability to build partnerships, manage complexity and mediate issues is required
Must have strong collaboration and influence management skills to partner effectively with Enterprise functions and across functions, and ability to build consensus and impact outcomes without always having line authority is required.
Proven track record of demonstrating collaboration across the organization and at all levels, and leading direct or indirect teams to deliver results is required.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills and experience implementing risk-based oversight programs are required.
Ability to balance technical understanding of products and processes with compliance perspective and business acumen is required.
Strong communication and executive presentation skills are required.
 

LOCATION

This position will be based in Japan and may require up to 50% travel both domestically and internationally.

#LI-Onsite 

========================================================

応募の際はAskGS内の社内公募ガイドラインをご一読いただき、応募ください。１８か月未満の方は応募前に上長及び担当BUHRの許可を取ってから応募ください。複数同時応募不可になります。

title: [A&V] Launch Excellence, Director
description:
Position Purpose 

The Launch Excellence Lead manages the Launch Excellence team and achieves the establishment of Launch Excellence pricetice in Japan. 

This role facilitates the successful launch of Pfizer breakthroughs in Japan, through cross-functional and global-local coordination as early as 3 years prior to launch to ensure that necessary steps (i.e., customer engagement, access plans, policy shaping) are taken ahead of time and properly resourced. 

 This role builds market expertise in launch planning and execution by developing best practices and incorporating them into future asset launches. 

The Launch Excellence Lead ensures that Japan's Launch Excellence Team is aligned with the Global Launch Excellence Team. 

Responsibilities 

Leads establishment of lanch excellence practice in Japan 

Partners with Country LT (TA leads, TA MA leads, DJ lead, and A&V lead) to prioritize launches across categories at the country/cluster level and coordinates resource allocation to support launch planning. 

Aligns with the global launch teams (Launch Navigation Group and Global DLPP Team) to exchange best-practices of launch excellence. 

Trains and coaches Launch Excellence Sr. Managers and secondment kolleagues from BUs to plan and execute launch preparation activites, and to develop their capabilities. 

 

Builds market expertise in launch planning and execution across BUs 

Encourages utilization of global tools, such as Digital Launch Planning Platform (DLPP), and resources by the global teams, to ensure consistent standards for launch preparation across BUs. 

Collects and establishes best practices for launch preparation activites. 

Incorporates new product planning into overall BU Cx experience plans working closely with H&V and Medical Launch team and/or respective BU medical teams. 

 

Facilitates the successful launch of Pfizer breakthroughs coordinating the cross-functional teams 

Leads the project management of new product launch preparations and coordinates the cross-functional teams (Cliniucal, Regulatory, Access, Marketing, Medical, PGS, Channel Strategy, IP, Legal, Compliance etc.) as early as 3 years prior to launch to ensure on-time execution.  

Serves as a conduit to a Global Asset Team, led by a Global Launch Operation Lead. 

Collaborates with medical/A&V (NPP, AS, Pricing, HEOR and RWE team) in the development of early engagement plans (KOLs, HCPs, PAGs, payers, patients), identification of local data generation needs and full understanding of the patient pathways to optimize and accelerate access. 

Ensures knowledge transfer to respective BU marketing team for smooth transition upon approval of commercialization. 

 

Desired Skill Set 

Must have Experience and Competency 

Strong and rich experience in Japanese pharmaceutical product launches 

At least 3years of marketing experience as brand manager or above, with diverse experience outside marketing is a plus 

Experience in leading in global organisational, cross-functional environment successfully 

Fluency in both Japanese (native level) and English (business discussion, writing, reading) 

Project management experience is a plus (i.e., strategic consultancy) 

 

Desired Experience and Competency 

Strategic thinking and analytical skills  

Agile thinking with flexible open mind 

Networking and Alliance building 

Active and positive communicator having a sense of inclusion 

Influencing without authority 

Organizational Relationships 

TA leads 

TA Brand Team 

TA MA leads 

TA MA Team 

DJ lead 

DJ Team (JCL, JRL, PPM, CMC, Dx Lead) 

A&V lead 

A&V Team (NPP, AS, Pricing, RWE, HEOR) 

Supply Team (PGS, Chanel Strategy) 

Direcrot of Strategy 

Finance 

Global launch excellence families 

Resource management or Management of resources  

2 full-time colleagues (Launch Excellence Sr, Manager and Manager) as play and coach model. 

Potentially secure resources through secondment programs, if necessary. 

 
Work Location Assignment: On Premise

Pfizer is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable equal employment opportunity legislation in each jurisdiction in which it operates.
